Do not quote or place tool-call markup inside explanatory text; when you decide to call the tool, emit the tool call itself.
If you use MOUSE, include integer row and col arguments.
EFFICIENCY BUDGET — your score on each level is (baseline_actions / your_actions)^2, so every wasted action costs you quadratically.
Level 2: you have used 51 actions; a strong score needs about 18 or fewer. You are 2.8x over the target.
NET-ZERO WASTE: your last 51 actions returned the board to a state already seen 51 actions ago — that round-trip made no progress yet still cost 51 actions. Do not extend-and-retract or oscillate; a sequence that ends where it began is pure waste.
If you are not steadily making progress toward the level goal, commit to your single best hypothesis and the shortest sequence that tests it — do not exhaustively scan rows/columns or enumerate every option. Test one idea, read the result, then decide.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Check current state
seg = current_frame.segmentation
print("Current state:")
for n in seg['nodes']:
    b = n['boundary']
    rows = [p[0] for p in b]
    cols = [p[1] for p in b]
    if n['color'] in ['W', 'b', 'P', 'c', 'N']:
        print(f"  color={n['color']} rows={min(rows)}-{max(rows)} cols={min(cols)}-{max(cols)} pixels={n['pixels']}")

print(f"\nStep: {current_frame.step}")
print(f"Level: {current_frame.level}")
print(f"Last action result: {last_action_result}")

# Try clicking blue block 2 to see if game still responds
action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 25, 'col': 1}])

seg = current_frame.segmentation
print("\nAfter blue block 2:")
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['color'] == 'W':
        b = n['boundary']
        cols = [p[1] for p in b]
        rows = [p[0] for p in b]
        print(f"  white rows={min(rows)}-{max(rows)} cols={min(cols)}-{max(cols)} width={max(cols)-min(cols)+1}")
print(f"Level completed: {last_action_result.get('level_completed', False)}")
print(f"Game over: {last_action_result.get('game_over', False)}")
print(f"Done: {last_action_result.get('done', False)}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
